messages.content: I am about to write a new piece on developer onboarding. What have I already published on onboarding, so the next piece builds on it instead of repeating it? Check my back catalogue.

[published 2026-01-01] "incident reviews field notes 0" (blog) — theme: incident reviews | take: blameless postmortems change behaviour more than dashboards
[published 2026-02-02] "on-call health field notes 1" (blog) — theme: on-call health | take: pay for sleep: a humane rotation beats heroics
[published 2026-03-03] "pricing strategy field notes 2" (thread) — theme: pricing strategy | take: usage-based pricing aligns incentives with infra buyers
[published 2026-04-04] "observability field notes 3" (talk) — theme: observability | take: reach for traces over logs once you pass a few services
[published 2026-05-05] "typescript field notes 4" (blog) — theme: typescript | take: parse, do not validate: put Zod at the edges
[published 2026-06-06] "caching field notes 5" (blog) — theme: caching | take: the cheapest request is the one you never send
[published 2026-02-11] "Onboarding in an afternoon" (blog) — theme: developer onboarding | take: a new hire should ship a real PR on day one, paired with a buddy and a pre-warmed devbox
[published 2026-08-08] "architecture field notes 7" (talk) — theme: architecture | take: boring technology is a feature, not an apology
[published 2026-09-09] "security field notes 8" (blog) — theme: security | take: rotate keys on a schedule, not after a breach
